# Common Mistakes — Anti-Patterns

**Anti-Pattern 1: Going for Perfection. You do not need to be perfect. Any improvement helps.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Relying on Supplements. Supplements do not replace whole foods.**

**Case: The Juice Cleanse. Juicing removes fiber. Eat whole fruits instead.**

**Case: The Gluten-Free Fad. Gluten is not the problem. Processed foods are.**

**Case: The Low-Carb Fallacy. Low-carb diets increase mortality. Complex carbohydrates are essential.**

**Case: The Protein Myth. Most people eat too much protein, not too little.**

**Case: The Dairy Myth. Dairy does not build strong bones. Plant sources of calcium are better absorbed.**

**Case: The Soy Fear. Soy is healthy. The fear of soy is not based on science.**

**Case: The Organic Myth. Organic junk food is still junk food. Focus on whole plant foods, not labels.**

**Case: The Detox. Your body detoxifies itself. You do not need juice cleanses or detox diets.**

**Case: The Complex Carbs. Carbohydrates from whole plant foods are essential for health. Simple sugars are the problem.**

**Case: The Small Changes. Starting with one meal per day can lead to lasting change.**

**Case: The Extreme Diet. An all-or-nothing approach fails. Start with small, sustainable changes.**

**Case: The Guilt Trap. Do not feel guilty about imperfect eating. Every healthy meal counts.**
